jobs.description:
Job summary
The Facilities Supervisor – Maintenance, will have a significant impact on customer experience. The Supervisor will have the ability to lead other technicians, design solutions for challenging problems, manage outside contractors, be on call for emergencies, to handle multiple projects and daily activities, and meet tight time lines.
The Supervisor will develop plans on how to accomplish engineering departmental goals and will be responsible for the leadership/development and coaching of the team through Performance Management.
This role also leads the technical team in providing both a reactive and pro-active maintenance service on all operational equipment and facilities infrastructure within the Amazon Facility.
The role has a direct impact on the site, its productivity and operational success. If equipment is not available or performs badly it minimizes the capacity and throughput of the site.

jobs.description:
Operations is the beating heart of Amazon. This key part of our business makes sure we fulfil and dispatch orders efficiently so that our customers get their items on time. It’s driven by Operations Managers who bring the team together to push excellence in safety, quality and productivity across our sites. At your site, you’ll lead other managers and create the operational plan that helps your teams meet their targets. This role is all about driving operational excellence to create the Amazon of tomorrow.


Key job responsibilities
- Manage and develop a team of Area Managers
- Lead operational teams on a shift, deal with issues, and positively impact site performance
- Analyse shift performance against relevant business objectives, and put in place actions to improve our operational excellence
- Work closely with other support teams, including HR, Finance, Health and Safety, and Security
- Design and deliver initiatives across the sites to improve operational performance


A day in the life
You’ll lead shifts at one of our operational sites. You’ll also be the key link between a number of teams, including finance, safety and operations integration teams. That means you’ll be close to the day-to-day operations at your site but also contribute to new ways to innovate at your site.

A typical day will involve meetings with different areas of the business, deep diving into performance and risk metrics for sites, and looking ahead to prepare for different levels of demand. Every day will also involve mentoring and coaching your team. You’ll be a source of leadership and support your team to be the best managers they can be.
jobs.description_short: Operations is the beating heart of Amazon. This key part of our business makes sure we fulfil and dispatch orders efficiently so that our customers get their items on time. It’s driven by Operations Managers

jobs.description:
The Central Programs Team, India (CPT India) team leads cross-functional projects that requires collaboration and partnership with Amazon businesses, geographical units and technical subject matter experts (SMEs). The projects are focused on initiatives to continually reduce risks and improve network WHS standards and procedures. Individuals gather business requirements, document functional and design specifications, identify appropriate resources needed, assemble the right project team, assign individual responsibilities and develop the milestones and launch schedules to ensure timely and successful delivery of the project. The team members measure and report progress, anticipate and resolve bottlenecks, provide escalation management, anticipate and make tradeoffs, and balance the business needs with the technical constraints. This a program management role responsible for executing per direction, the management of the WW WHS programs (standards, procedures, best practices) development, training and continuous improvement projects. The role involves hands-on work in the areas of understanding stakeholder needs and expectations, WHS regulatory research, global stakeholder engagement, data analytics and document technical writing. The candidate must be a self-starter and detail-oriented. They must be an effective communicator and send clear, concise and consistent messages, both verbally and in writing.

Key job responsibilities
Program/Process Improvement, Project Management
• Clearly and timely communicate findings, determinations, and recommendations to compliance management and business partners, both at periodic intervals and as needed regarding escalated or high-risk compliance issues.
• Guide management in the development/review of applicable policies, procedures and business practices.
Engage in frequent written and verbal communication with management and business partners to accomplish goals.
• Execute and drive audits to completion per SOP. This includes drafting audit reports, stakeholder reviews of audit reports, finalizing and tracking audit reports in database and tracking issues in system (and SIM/TT management).
• Owns weekly/monthly reports and metrics.
• Identifies gaps in audit programs and processes and escalates to manager.
• Follows confidentiality rules with the documents reviewed.
• Drafts documents and revisions on audit reports per manager direction.
• Performs deep dive analysis/research on data/information/literature and creates recommendations/corrective actions based on identified deviations and recommends appropriate solutions.
• Earns trust of peers by understanding audit processes and programs.
• Makes recommendations to managers for input into roadmap strategic discussions and continuous improvement projects to drive program efficiencies.
jobs.description_short: The Central Programs Team, India (CPT India) team leads cross-functional projects that requires collaboration and partnership with Amazon businesses, geographical units and technical subject matter experts

jobs.description:
Amazon Extra Large (AMXL) is looking for a customer focused, analytically and technically skilled Business Intelligence Engineer to drive effective performance measurement and management, innovation and execution in last mile delivery. This position will be responsible for building out analysis and visualization tools and processes’ to support our growing Amazon Logistics business in Europe.

The successful candidate will be able to effectively retrieve, integrate, visualize and present critical data to improve the efficiency of the package delivery network, and enable efficient management of a large global logistics system. This individual will work with technical and business teams to optimize planning, execution, process improvement and track progress against goals. This role requires an individual with excellent analytical abilities as well as outstanding business acumen and comfort with technical teams and systems. The successful candidate will be a self-starter comfortable with ambiguity, with strong attention to detail, and enjoy working with large scale of data.



Key job responsibilities
- Maintain and develop data pipeline for EU region
- Automate and improve existing manual processes
- Create tools to help deep diving and investigations into operations execution and business problems
- Analyze and visualize large scale logistics and transaction data to determine user behavior or delivery process problems, and output solid analysis report with recommendation.
jobs.description_short: Amazon Extra Large (AMXL) is looking  for a customer focused, analytically and technically skilled Business  Intelligence Engineer to drive effective performance measurement and  management, innovation

- Experience with statistical analysis, co-relation analysis

- Experience with planning, performance metrics, and process improvement
jobs.description:
Amazon is seeking a dynamic and customer-obsessed Supervisors to lead and develop teams within our fast-paced AMZL (Amazon Logistics) and FC (Fulfilment Enter) environments. This role will encompass responsibilities across both areas, requiring a versatile leader who can thrive in a dynamic, ever-changing operational landscape. You will play a crucial role in ensuring efficient workflows, exceeding customer expectations, and driving continuous improvement. This is an opportunity to make history with one of the world's largest and most innovative companies, shaping the future of operations and logistics.

Key job responsibilities
Operational Leadership: Oversee daily operations within both AMZL and FC environments, including managing teams of Operations Supervisors, Operations Assistants, Delivery Service Providers, and associates (ranging from approximately 30-40). This includes allocating labor, leading meetings, assigning job duties, and maintaining exceptional organizational skills.
Team Management: Lead, train, and develop high-performing teams. Foster a positive and motivating work environment, driving engagement and performance while ensuring associate safety and adherence to Amazon's Health and Safety standards.
Performance Management: Be obsessed with metrics, diving deep to identify root causes of performance issues, and implementing effective solutions. Continuously monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and drive improvements across both AMZL and FC processes.
Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to streamline and improve processes. Find practical and simple solutions to complex problems without compromising quality or functionality. Utilize problem-solving and analytical skills to drive Dive Deep analyses and implement process enhancements. Become a subject matter expert on core operational processes.
Communication & Collaboration: Communicate daily metrics and report exceptions. Effectively communicate with business and technical audiences, influencing decisions and driving alignment. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to solve operational challenges and share best practices. Manage external agency relationships and performance.
Problem Solving & Decision Making: Demonstrate strong problem-solving and analytical capabilities. Act decisively in a fast-paced environment, exercising sound judgment and making informed decisions.
Technology & Tools: Continuously utilize software and hardware tools to ensure smooth daily operations. Ensure associates have the necessary tools and coordinate repairs or replacements as needed. Support the rollout and implementation of new systems and processes, gathering feedback and providing support.
Inventory Management: Maintain inventory accuracy within the FC environment.

jobs.description:
Do you want to join the Amazon Smart Security team in Europe? We are looking for a customer-obsessed, analytical, results-oriented Product Manager to drive Online sales and marketing initiatives for Amazon Ring and Amazon blink security devices.

You'll get to leverage your online marketing and selling experience to make a real impact. You'll analyze data and propose recommendations that drive results. You'll have the opportunity to create demand for our business and category, working strategically while also diving deep into implementation when needed—planning online marketing campaigns, coordinating promotional activities, writing business requirements, and analyzing data. You'll take ownership of your work and contribute to our high-energy, high-performance culture.

*Please note, this is a 12 month fixed term contract.

Key job responsibilities
- Support the smart security business towards its full year sales goals in the Amazon online channel
- Help to plan and execute a full year promotional plan
- Manage forecasting for their products in order to ensure adequate supply
- Optimize key discoverability inputs
- Report on business performance on weekly basis to senior leadership
- Work with internal teams to develop merchandising and testing plans

A day in the life
In this highly visible role, you will manage online sales and marketing levers for Ring or Blink products, working closely with global and local sales, partner teams, site merchandising and advertising teams across Amazon to ensure the business reaches its maximum potential.

About the team
Our team owns sales and marketing of Ring, Blink, smart home products on Amazon websites. We leverage best practices across Amazon Devices teams to ensure sales growth, provide a great customer experience, and drive innovation.

- Experience measuring key operational metrics (e.g., order cycle time, ship estimate accuracy, on-time and complete shipment, inventory record defect rate)

- Experience working with data mining on large datasets
- Experience within business analyst, data analyst or similar role (including internships)
jobs.description:
Our team, Amazon Now (Quick Commerce) – MENA, supports the rapid-delivery grocery and convenience business across the Middle East and North Africa. We partner closely with regional stakeholders across Operations, Supply Chain, Vendor Management, Instock, Pricing, Marketing, and Last-Mile Delivery to drive best-in-class customer experience in ultra-fast commerce.

The ideal candidate will perform deep-dive analyses to identify root causes of operational inefficiencies, develop data-backed recommendations, and build dashboards and reporting mechanisms that enable leadership to monitor performance in near real time. This role will also support forecasting and capacity planning to ensure service-level targets are met while balancing cost and operational efficiency.

You will work closely with regional and country teams to ensure data accuracy, surface performance trends, and drive continuous improvement initiatives across Amazon Now markets.

Key job responsibilities
Design, build, and maintain operational dashboards and reporting to track core Quick Commerce metrics (e.g., service levels, speed, efficiency, capacity utilization)

Support short-term and mid-term planning and forecasting for demand, capacity, and operational resources

Drive visibility into actual vs expected performance, enabling real-time and retrospective performance management

Monitor and analyze efficiency and quality metrics across stores, regions, and countries

Conduct root-cause analyses on performance gaps and operational misses, and quantify business impact

Partner with Operations Managers (OMs), Program Managers (PMs), and central teams to support business reviews, WBRs, and deep dives

Communicate insights clearly to senior stakeholders through executive-ready narratives and recommendations

Ensure data quality, consistency, and metric definitions across reporting and analytical outputs

Proactively identify trends and risks that may impact customer experience, speed, or cost

Develop and recommend process optimization and efficiency improvements across the Amazon Now operating model

Work autonomously in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple priorities and timelines

jobs.description:
At Amazon, we strive to be Earth’s most customer-centric company where people can find and discover anything they want to buy online. We hire the world’s brightest minds, offering them an environment in which they can relentlessly improve the experience for customers. Innovation and creativity are built into the DNA of the company and are encouraged at all levels of employment. Every day we solve complex technical and business problems with ingenuity and simplicity. We’re making history and the good news is we’ve only just begun

About the team
In Inbound, we are currently a team of 7 Area Managers, 5 Team Leads and 12 Process Assistants. Inbound process typically starts from unloading at dock till stowing in shelving area.This is followed by stowing of the shipment units in specific process paths using the recommended GL type.

jobs.description:
Our Financial Operations team is now looking for a proactive, customer-focused and detail-oriented Finance Analyst whose primary responsibility will be to manage and resolve receivables and payment-related queries received from our business customers. This role combines credit collection operations with cash application responsibilities, requiring strong collaboration across multiple teams and departments.
The successful candidate will be responsible for cash collection operations in cooperation with our local sales and finance management, while also managing cash application activities including reconciliation, and resolution of unapplied cash receipts.

Your key responsibilities:
• Manage customer liabilities to Amazon - credit collection operations and monitor AR aging reports and collections targets
• Respond to customer invoicing inquiries, research unpaid or disputed amounts and work directly with customers to resolve their issues and correct invoicing errors
• Continually look at ways to improve customer experience and customer satisfaction
• Maintain, prepare, and distribute reports to supervisors and stakeholders
• Achieve monthly productivity and accuracy targets
• Collaborate with internal and external teams to drive process improvements and resolve escalations and discrepancies
• Diagnose, improve and fix issues with current collections and cash application processes
• Reconcile Amazon’s open receivables with customers to ensure quick and timely AR collection
• Earn the trust of external and internal stakeholders/customers, build long-lasting relationships that reinforce Amazon’s customer centricity

Base pay for this position starts from 1,350+ Eur gross per month and salary depends on the skills and requirements. Company benefits apply subject to further terms, including annual membership in medical clinic, life insurance, retirement pension, meal voucher card.
